rulings.Case Title: J&R SAN FRANCISCO, INC. et al VS. CHO QUON FUNG et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Meredith Fondahl'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Discovery Requests And Production Of Documents By J And R San Francisco, Inc.
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday June 2, 2016, line 1. Defendant Meredith Fondahl'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Discovery Requests And Production Of Documents By J And R San Francisco, Inc. GRANTED in Part and DENIED in Part as follows: 1. Plaintiff is ordered to serve on Defendant within fifteen (15) days from the date of this order further responses to Request for Production No. 20 for the period June/July 2011; Request for Production No. 23, subject to redaction of all clearly attorney client information, but with sufficient information to show the type of work performed and the specific aspect of the dispute to which it related. Plaintiff's complaints have raised issues which implicate the information contained in its billing records and the fee schedule provided is inadequate to allow defendant to meet those claims. 2. All other requests are denied either as moot or Plaintiff's responses are adequate. 3. Sanctions in the amount of $4,500.00 will be assessed against Plaintiff and its attorneys with payment to be made within ten (10) days from the date of this order. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: JAMES D BANKS VS. FIRST AMERICAN TITLE INSURANCE COMPANY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Order To Show Cause Why A Preliminary Injunction Should Not Be Issued
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday June 2, 2016, line 2. Order To Show Cause Why A Preliminary Injunction Should Not Be Issued DENIED, moving party failed to sustain his burden and show probability of prevailing on the merits. Specifically, Plaintiff filed to cite law or present evidence as to the damages caused by the recordation of NOD, i.e. that he was prevented from applying for additional extension as a result of NOD being recorded. = (501/REQ)

Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday June 2, 2016, line 5. DEFENDANT SERGIO HERNANDEZ MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT GRANTED, subject to opposition and Defendant providing proof of service executed by a person who accomplished service. Proof of Service by Ryan Murphy is insufficient. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: LINDA M. ELSDON VS. RODERIC CRANSTON ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday June 2, 2016, line 3. DEFENDANT RODERIC CRANSTON DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT OFF CALENDAR, First Amended Complaint filed on May 18, 2016. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: JULIA VARGAS VS. LORENA TECUATL ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday June 2, 2016, line 4. DEFENDANT LORENA TECUATL, ELIZABETH AGUILAR DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. Complaint is sufficiently alleged and is not uncertain. Defendants raise factual issues not appropriate on demurrer. Plaintiff's counsel is ordered to comply with SFLR 2.7B in future. Failure to comply with result in motion being granted as unopposed. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: YUE YING XU VS. MAN ZHU JIANG ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday June 2, 2016, line 6. DEFENDANT MAN JIANG, MING LAU MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT GRANTED, subject to opposition. = (501/REQ)
